steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Before the blank is an independent clause ending with 'thousands.' After the blank, 'that the once-revolutionary dual-core technology...can be found in everyday items' is a dependent clause expressing the consequence or result of that claim. The punctuation must correctly connect the two.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'thousands—that': em-dash before 'that' correctly introduces a consequence clause. ✓
  • Choice B — em-dash after 'that': misplaces the em-dash, breaking the dependent clause mid-word. ✗
  • Choice C — period: creates a sentence fragment ('that the once-revolutionary...' cannot stand alone). ✗
  • Choice D — comma: a comma is too weak to introduce a consequence clause after an independent clause here. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
